summary: Full backend + frontend test run for Brushly. 14/14 pytest tests passing against the public preview URL. Playwright UI tests passing on desktop (1280x900) and mobile (414x896): brand, streak, morning/evening logging with toasts, calendar coloring level-1 -> level-2, per-day (not per-event) streak logic, month nav, reload persistence, and no horizontal overflow on mobile.
critical_code_review_comments: Naive datetime usage: BrushEvent.timestamp uses datetime.now() (local naive). If the server is in a different TZ than the user, 'today' in /api/streak (server side, date.today()) and the JS 'today' (client side, new Date()) can disagree near midnight. Consider storing UTC and computing 'today' from a client-supplied date or X-Timezone header. Not blocking for MVP.

GET '/' through the Kubernetes ingress in this preview env is routed to a separate React container, not to FastAPI. The FastAPI '@app.get("/")' handler works locally on :8001 (verified) but is unreachable via the public URL. This is an env quirk, not a code bug; /api/ correctly serves the Brushly HTML.

/api/history validates month bounds but the year check only catches absurd ranges (1970..9999); year=2025, month=2 with day=30 etc. is fine here because we only use year/month for range. No real issue.
init_db() is called at module import time. Acceptable for SQLite + this scale, but for multi-worker setups prefer an explicit FastAPI startup event.
CORS is wide-open (allow_origins=['*']); fine for this MVP.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all critical flows: load, brand, streak, morning/evening, calendar coloring level-1/level-2, per-day streak, prev/next nav, reload persistence, mobile no-overflow, button tap-size)

seed_data_creation: Cleared /app/brushly.db SQLite table before the UI run. Backend test suite repopulates a few rows; final state after the full run: 2 events on today's date.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use BASE_URL=https://floss-flow.preview.emergentagent.com. Frontend lives at BASE_URL/api/ (NOT root) due to ingress routing root -> React container. Pytest file: /app/backend/tests/test_brushly.py. To reset state: `python -c "from sqlmodel import Session, delete; from database import engine; from models import BrushEvent;\nimport sys;\nfrom sqlalchemy import text;" -- or simply: from sqlmodel import Session, delete; with Session(engine) as s: s.exec(delete(BrushEvent)); s.commit()`. The 'today' day cell testid format is day-YYYY-MM-DD; coloring classes are level-0|1|2 plus 'today'.
